---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables (Using Endline Scores to show final impact)
  outcome_var: "endline_score"
  group_var: "treatment_group"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  treatment_label: "Received Program"
  control_label: "Comparison Group"
  metric_name: "Endline Test Score"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Treatment Effects: Forest Plot"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 3.5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the required variables
  drop_na(!!sym(params$outcome_var), !!sym(params$group_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub and standardize text inputs
  mutate(
    Clean_Group = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$group_var)))),
    
    # Intelligently map messy data to the clean Policy Labels defined in YAML
    Group = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Group, "(?i)Treat|Intervention|1|Yes|Received") ~ str_wrap(params$treatment_label, width = 20),
      TRUE ~ str_wrap(params$control_label, width = 20)
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Group data to calculate statistics efficiently
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarize(
    Estimate = mean(!!sym(params$outcome_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(!!sym(params$outcome_var), na.rm = TRUE),
    N = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 4. Calculate Standard Error and 95% Confidence Intervals
  mutate(
    SE = SD / sqrt(N),
    Lower_CI = Estimate - (1.96 * SE),
    Upper_CI = Estimate + (1.96 * SE)
  ) %>%
  
  # 5. Lock factor levels so the Treatment group always plots on top
  mutate(
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c(
      str_wrap(params$treatment_label, width = 20), 
      str_wrap(params$control_label, width = 20)
    ))
  )
```


```{r plot-effects}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

# Create a named color vector dynamically based on the wrapped YAML labels
group_colors <- setNames(
  c(unname(cerp_cols["primary"]), unname(cerp_cols["neutral"])), 
  c(str_wrap(params$treatment_label, width = 20), str_wrap(params$control_label, width = 20))
)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Estimate, y = Group, color = Group)) +
  
  # Draw the 95% Confidence Interval error bars
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Lower_CI, xmax = Upper_CI), height = 0, linewidth = 2) +
  
  # Draw the point estimate (the average)
  geom_point(size = 7) +
  
  # Map the colors explicitly so treatment is primary and control is neutral
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Remove the legend entirely and clean axes
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Estimated Impact on", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("Points represent the average outcome. Lines represent the 95% confidence interval.\nIf the horizontal lines do not overlap, the difference is statistically significant."),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (params$metric_name),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
